fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Oklahoma Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Oklahoma - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity oklahoma, how to transfer dietitian license to oklahoma, dietitian compact oklahoma, oklahoma dietitian endorsement application

board.name: Oklahoma Licensed Dietitians Committee
board.parent_dept: Oklahoma State Board of Medical Licensure and Supervision

quick_facts.fee_breakdown: $120 initial application fee (background check fees extra)

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian (LD)
license_types.description: The credential required to practice dietetics in the state of Oklahoma. Supervised by the Licensed Dietitians Committee under the Medical Board. Note: Oklahoma does not license the title 'Nutritionist'.

license_types.title: Provisional Licensed Dietitian (PLD)
license_types.description: Available for applicants who have completed the academic and supervised practice requirements but have not yet passed the CDR examination. Must practice under the supervision of a Licensed Dietitian. Valid for one year.

steps.title: Complete Required Education and Supervised Practice
steps.description: You must complete a didactic program in dietetics and supervised practice accredited by the Accreditation Council for Education in Nutrition and Dietetics (ACEND).

steps.title: Pass the Registration Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration Examination.

steps.title: Apply for an Oklahoma Dietitian License
steps.description: Submit an application to the Oklahoma Licensed Dietitians Committee with the required documentation and fees.

documents.detail: Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration sent directly to the Board.
Sent directly from the university showing your conferred degree.
Processed through the Oklahoma State Bureau of Investigation (OSBI).
Form documenting your citizenship or qualified alien status.

provisional_text: Oklahoma offers a Provisional Licensed Dietitian (PLD) distinct credential for applicants who have met the education and supervised practice requirements and are preparing for the CDR exam. A Provisional Licensed Dietitian must practice under the direct supervision of an Oklahoma Licensed Dietitian. The provisional license is valid for one year.
reciprocity_text: Applicants currently licensed in another state may apply via endorsement if the other state's licensing requirements are substantially equivalent to or stricter than Oklahoma's. The standard application, fee, and verification of all out-of-state licenses are required.

faqs.question: How much does an Oklahoma dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial license application fee is $120. Additional fees apply for the required background check.

faqs.question: When does the Oklahoma license need to be renewed?
faqs.answer: The Oklahoma licensed dietitian credential expires annually on October 31st. The renewal fee is $100.

faqs.question: Does Oklahoma license Nutritionists?
faqs.answer: No, Oklahoma only issues licenses for Dietitians (Licensed Dietitian). There are no specific licensing requirements or title protections for individuals claiming to be 'nutritionists' in the state.

faqs.question: Does Oklahoma have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: Oklahoma has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, dietitians still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Oklahoma?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Oklahoma while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Applicants currently licensed in another state may apply via endorsement if the other state's licensing requirements are substantially equivalent to or stricter than Oklahoma's. The standard application, fee, and verification of all out-of-state licenses are required.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in Oklahoma
hero.dek: Oklahoma Dietitian License requires completing an accredited dietetics program, passing the Commission on Dietetic Registration Examination, and meeting continuing education requirements.
